content.problemStatement: Given two integers **N** and **M**, find how many permutations of **1, 2, ..., N** (first **N** natural numbers) are there where the sum of **every two adjacent** numbers is at most **M**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and M.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of permutations modulo 1000000007 on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the valid permutations are {2,3,1,4}, {3,2,1,4}, {4,1,2,3}, and {4,1,3,2}. For the second test case, there are 72 valid permutations.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the number of permutations of the first i numbers where the last number is j. We iterate over all possible values of j and update dp[i][j] by adding the number of permutations of the first i-1 numbers where the last number is k and j+k+1 <= M.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) per test case, O(T * N^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the 2D dp array.
